invariants.name: Ingress contract — SID worker and MID engine have typed inputs
invariants.evidence: mid-engine.ts reads LcsSidOutputRow from lcs.sid_output (CONSTRUCTED). sid-worker.ts reads LcsCidRow from lcs.cid (COMPILED). Both typed via @/data/lcs imports.

invariants.name: Egress contract — adapter payload contract covers all Lovable request fields
invariants.evidence: AdapterPayload (16 fields) maps to LovableRequestPayload (15 fields) in lovable-delivery-adapter.ts. All lovable_contract.request_fields from WP covered: communication_id, message_run_id, channel, recipient_email, recipient_linkedin_url, subject_line, body_plain, body_html, sender_identity, frame_id, lifecycle_phase, sovereign_company_id, company_name, agent_number, metadata.

invariants.name: No orphan tables — no new tables introduced
invariants.evidence: No DB changes in this work packet. Adapter rewire is code-only.

invariants.name: No unconsumed events — webhook return path verified
invariants.evidence: 3 webhook functions (lcs-mailgun-webhook, lcs-heyreach-webhook, lcs-inbound-reply) all write to CET (lcs.event) at step 8/9 and emit engagement/reply signals to lcs.signal_queue. Return path intact and verified.

invariants.name: ID propagation — communication_id and message_run_id flow through adapter to webhook
invariants.evidence: LovableDeliveryAdapter passes communication_id and message_run_id in request payload. Webhooks extract these from event data (Mailgun user-variables, HeyReach metadata) to correlate with original CET events.
